channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Open-Sources RAMPART and Clarity to Secure AI Agents During Development
item.description:
Microsoft has unveiled two new open-source tools called RAMPART and Clarity to assist developers in better testing the security of artificial intelligence (AI) agents.
RAMPART, short for Risk Assessm...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Patch Now: Critical Flaw in OT Robot OS Gives Attackers Control
item.description: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit the command injection vulnerability to gain remote access to robotic systems, causing significant disruption to the environment....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Grafana breach caused by missed token rotation after TanStack attack
item.description: The Grafana data breach was caused by a single GitHub workflow token that slipped through the rotation process following the TanStack npm supply-chain attack last week. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Takes Down Malware-Signing Service Behind Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday said it disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that weaponized the company's Artifact Signing system to deliver malicious code and conduct ransomware and other...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Identity Alone Isn't Enough: Why Device Security Has to Share the Load
item.description: Identity checks alone can't stop attackers using stolen session tokens and compromised devices. Specops Software outlines why Zero Trust strategies increasingly depend on continuous device verificatio...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Drupal critical update to fix bug with high exploitation risk
item.description: Drupal has announced a "core security release" scheduled for later today, warning that threat actors might develop exploits within hours of the update disclosure. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Webworm Deploys EchoCreep and GraphWorm Backdoors Using Discord and MS Graph API
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged fresh activity from a China-aligned threat actor known as Webworm in 2025, deploying custom backdoors that employ Discord and Microsoft Graph API for command-and...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
item.description:
New Industry Data Just Released Suggests Not.
On May 19th, 2026, Orchid Security released the results of our Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026. Among the findings, "identity dark matter" (the unseen, unman...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GitHub Breached — Employee Device Hack Led to Exfiltration of 3,800+ Internal Repos
item.description: GitHub on Tuesday said it's investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after the notorious threat actor known as TeamPCP listed the platform's source code and internal organization...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Exploit released for new PinTheft Arch Linux root escalation flaw
item.description: PinTheft, a recently patched Linux privilege escalation vulnerability, now has a publicly available proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit that allows local attackers to gain root privileges on Arch Linux sys...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Typosquatting Is No Longer a User Problem. It's a Supply Chain Problem
item.description: AI-generated lookalike domains are now embedded inside the third-party scripts running on your web properties. Here's why your current stack can't see them, and what detection actually requires. Downl...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Releases Mitigation for YellowKey BitLocker Bypass CVE-2026-45585 Exploit
item.description:
Microsoft on Tuesday released a mitigation for a BitLocker bypass vulnerability named YellowKey following its public disclosure last week.
The zero-day flaw, now tracked as CVE-2026-45585, carries a ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GitHub confirms breach of 3,800 repos via malicious VSCode extension
item.description: GitHub has confirmed that roughly 3,800 internal repositories were breached after one of its employees installed a malicious VS Code extension. [...]...

item.title: Microsoft shares mitigation for YellowKey Windows zero-day
item.description: Microsoft has shared mitigations for YellowKey, a recently disclosed Windows BitLocker zero-day vulnerability that grants access to protected drives. [...]...

item.title: Interpol's 'Operation Ramz' Pioneers Cross-Region Collabs in Middle East
item.description: While the numbers are modest, the crackdown on cybercrime involved 13 countries in the MENA region, the largest law enforcement collaboration to date....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Grafana GitHub Breach Exposes Source Code via TanStack npm Attack
item.description:
Grafana Labs, on May 19, 2026, said an investigation into its recent breach found no evidence of customer production systems or operations being compromised.
It said the scope of the incident is limi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GitHub investigates internal repositories breach claimed by TeamPCP
item.description: GitHub is investigating a breach of its internal repositories after the TeamPCP hacker group claimed to have accessed approximately 4,000 repositories containing private code. [...]...

item.title: Max-severity flaw in ChromaDB for AI apps allows server hijacking
item.description: A max-severity vulnerability in the latest Python FastAPI version of the ChromaDB project allows unauthenticated attackers to run arbitrary code on exposed servers. [...]...

item.title: What Will Make AI BOMs Real?
item.description: A brief overview of the forces at play that will get more organizations on board with creating and consuming AI bill of materials (BOMs)....

item.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
item.description: Verizon's 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys....

item.title: Cybercrime service disrupted for abusing Microsoft platform to sign malware
item.description: Microsoft says it has disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that abused the company's Artifact Signing service to generate fraudulent code-signing certificates used by ransomware ...

item.title: Discord rolls out end-to-end encryption on voice, video calls
item.description: Discord announced that all voice and video calls through the communication platform are now protected by default with end-to-end encryption (E2EE). [...]...

item.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
item.description: The agency's GitHub repository, publicly available since November 2025, was ironically named "Private-CISA."...

item.title: Stealer Spoofs Google, Microsoft & Apple, Then Backdoors macOS
item.description: The SHub Reaper stealer, which hides behind fake WeChat and Miro installers, marks a shift from ClickFix social engineering to Apple script-based execution....

item.title: FBI: Americans lost over $388 million to scams using crypto ATMs in 2025
item.description: The FBI says Americans have lost over $388 million last year to scams using cryptocurrency kiosks, also known as crypto ATMs or Bitcoin ATMs. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset abused in Azure data theft attacks
item.description: A threat actor targeting Microsoft 365 and Azure production environments is stealing data in attacks that abuse legitimate applications and administration features. [...]...
